facts.text: Lake Lorraine was once a freshwater lake until the 1990s when a sandbar breach connected it to Choctawhatchee Bay, turning it into a brackish lagoon.
The community grew around Country Club Road in the 1970s, circling a golf course that still defines the neighborhood’s layout.
Lake Lorraine is part of the Crestview–Fort Walton Beach–Destin Metropolitan Statistical Area, linking it to a larger regional economy.
In the 1990s and 2000s, Black's Point was transformed into a residential street grid named after famous golfers, reflecting the area’s golf heritage.
During World War II, Eglin Field’s target float in Choctawhatchee Bay near Black's Point served as a battleship‑size training platform for naval crews.
The CDP’s geographic center sits at 30.4416 N, 86.5652 W, placing it firmly within Okaloosa County’s Central time zone.
